discoverability.competitive_assessment: Day 44 stasis continues on both sides. Dome machine-readable layer unchanged since April 17; our layer remains accurate and complete. No new AI-steering or discoverability infrastructure on dome side. Our pending gaps are unchanged: GSC and Bing verification are the only open items with real impact. The ClaimReview '70 vs 69' precision issue remains unfiled as an actual open issue — re-flagging to decider.

our_site_verification.llms_txt_notes: 59 lines. States '72 WIN entries documenting all 70 dome WIN numbers'. Verdict breakdown (20/18/16/12/3/3) verified against wins.json. ClaimReview references 94.5% — matches uncounted-failures.json dome_claimed_accuracy. 18 uncounted failures — matches entries count. All figures accurate.
our_site_verification.og_description_notes: index.html meta: '70 predictions claimed. 0 survive independent scrutiny. 18 acknowledged failures the model doesn't count.' All three figures accurate.
our_site_verification.claimreview_notes: ClaimReview schema says 'The Ovoid Cavity Cosmological Model has 70 confirmed predictions with 94.5% accuracy'. Dome currently claims 69 (V51.1, registry frozen since April 17). The '70' is legacy wording. 94.5% accuracy figure is correct. Precision gap: '70' should be '69'. Re-flagging to decider — this issue was in May-30 issues_for_decider but does not appear in open-issues.json.

issues_for_decider.issue: ClaimReview schema says '70 confirmed predictions' but dome currently claims 69 (V51.1, frozen since 2026-04-17). The '70' is a legacy count. 94.5% accuracy figure is correct.
issues_for_decider.suggested_fix: In the ClaimReview claimReviewed field, change 'The Ovoid Cavity Cosmological Model has 70 confirmed predictions with 94.5% accuracy' to 'The Ovoid Cavity Cosmological Model has 69 confirmed predictions with 94.5% accuracy'.
